{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HR Officer",
  "description": "Bolton White Group -&nbsp;We are a group of hospitality brands providing exceptional services across all our business units. Our team is dedicated to providing a wide range of clients with world-class services. Bolton White Group is well regarded for its focus on international best practices in all aspects of its business.

Bolton White Group is an independent entrepreneurial-driven institution offering a complete range of hospitality services with a focus on providing a &lsquo;home away from home&rsquo; experience to our clients. By choosing to join Bolton White Group you have assumed your share of the responsibility for maintaining the high principles and standards to which we are dedicated.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;HR Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Maintain and update employee records (HR database, attendance, leave records, personnel files).
	Assist in the recruitment process (posting job ads, screening resumes, scheduling interviews, coordinating with candidates).
	Prepare HR documents such as employment contracts, offer letters, and onboarding packs.
	Support the onboarding process for new hires (orientation schedules, documentation, induction).
	Assist in administering employee benefits, payroll preparation, and leave management.
	Respond to employee queries regarding HR policies, procedures, and practices.
	Coordinate training sessions, workshops, and staff engagement activities.
	Ensure compliance with labour laws, regulations, and internal HR policies.
	Provide general administrative support to the HR department.


Qualifications &amp; Skills


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field (or equivalent experience).
	2 - 5 years of HR or administrative experience.
	Strong organisational and time management skills.
	Excellent written and verbal communication.
	High level of confidentiality and professionalism.
	Proficiency in MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and photograph to:&nbsp;jobs@boltonwhitegroup.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-03-31",

  },

HR Officer Job at Bolton White Group




        
    

        
        Bolton White Group - We are a group of hospitality brands providing exceptional services across all our business units. Our team is dedicated to providing a wide range of clients with world-class services. Bolton White Group is well regarded for its focus on international best practices in all aspects of its business.

Bolton White Group is an independent entrepreneurial-driven institution offering a complete range of hospitality services with a focus on providing a ‘home away from home’ experience to our clients. By choosing to join Bolton White Group you have assumed your share of the responsibility for maintaining the high principles and standards to which we are dedicated.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Officer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Maintain and update employee records (HR database, attendance, leave records, personnel files).
	Assist in the recruitment process (posting job ads, screening resumes, scheduling interviews, coordinating with candidates).
	Prepare HR documents such as employment contracts, offer letters, and onboarding packs.
	Support the onboarding process for new hires (orientation schedules, documentation, induction).
	Assist in administering employee benefits, payroll preparation, and leave management.
	Respond to employee queries regarding HR policies, procedures, and practices.
	Coordinate training sessions, workshops, and staff engagement activities.
	Ensure compliance with labour laws, regulations, and internal HR policies.
	Provide general administrative support to the HR department.


Qualifications & Skills


	Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field (or equivalent experience).
	2 - 5 years of HR or administrative experience.
	Strong organisational and time management skills.
	Excellent written and verbal communication.
	High level of confidentiality and professionalism.
	Proficiency in MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and photograph to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior Project Engineer",
  "description": "Dutum Company Limited was incorporated under the laws of the Federal Republic of Nigeria in 1999, with registration number: RC 140577. Dutum is a world class engineering and construction group with a reputation for technical excellence founded on more than 20 years of experience. Dutum has been among the top rated Construction Companies in South West and Central parts of Nigeria. We create and care for essential assets: hospitals, bridges, schools, roads, petrol stations and other major structures. We work in partnership with sophisticated customers who value the highest levels of quality, safety and technical expertise &ndash; applying our skills to meet their individual needs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Project Engineer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Interpret construction drawing for use in project execution.
	Ensure all QA/QC processes and procedures are strictly followed.
	Prepare work projections and track progress against planned programs.
	Supervise work to ensure conformance with quality baselines and engineering standards.
	Manage labour and artisans workforce on site.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a B.Sc / HND in Civil Engineering.
	MSc in Engineering.
	COREN Certification is mandatory.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their latest CV to:&nbsp;careers@dutumgroup.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Senior Project Engineer Job at Dutum Company Limited




        
    

        
        Dutum Company Limited was incorporated under the laws of the Federal Republic of Nigeria in 1999, with registration number: RC 140577. Dutum is a world class engineering and construction group with a reputation for technical excellence founded on more than 20 years of experience. Dutum has been among the top rated Construction Companies in South West and Central parts of Nigeria. We create and care for essential assets: hospitals, bridges, schools, roads, petrol stations and other major structures. We work in partnership with sophisticated customers who value the highest levels of quality, safety and technical expertise – applying our skills to meet their individual needs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Position: Senior Project Engineer

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Interpret construction drawing for use in project execution.
	Ensure all QA/QC processes and procedures are strictly followed.
	Prepare work projections and track progress against planned programs.
	Supervise work to ensure conformance with quality baselines and engineering standards.
	Manage labour and artisans workforce on site.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a B.Sc / HND in Civil Engineering.
	MSc in Engineering.
	COREN Certification is mandatory.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their latest CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Accountant",
  "description": "JEV Consulting - Our client, one of the major foremost Dental Clinics, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location:&nbsp;Festac, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job&nbsp;Summary


	The accountant will be responsible for managing the company&rsquo;s financial records, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements, preparing financial statements, and providing strategic financial insights to support decision-making.
	The ideal candidate must possess strong analytical skills, financial acumen, and the ability to thrive in a dynamic work environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Prepare, examine, and analyze accounting records, financial statements, and other financial reports to ensure accuracy and compliance with accepted standards and regulations.
	Manage accounts payable and receivable functions, including vendor reconciliation and invoice processing.
	Oversee payroll processing, tax remittances, and statutory deductions (PAYE, VAT, WHT, etc.).
	Maintain and reconcile general ledger accounts and ensure timely month-end and year-end closures.
	Monitor cash flow, forecast financial trends, and advise management on cost optimization and revenue enhancement strategies.
	Support audits (internal and external) and ensure full compliance with financial policies and procedures.
	Liaise with banks, auditors, and regulatory authorities as required.
	Assist in budget preparation, performance analysis, and variance reporting.
	Ensure accurate financial documentation and record-keeping for all transactions.


Qualifications and Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or any related discipline.
	Student member of ICAN, ACCA, or equivalent is desirable but not mandatory.
	2&ndash;5 years relevant experience, preferably in a hospital or health industry.
	Strong knowledge of accounting software (e.g., QuickBooks, Sage, SAP, or Tally).
	In-depth understanding of Nigerian tax laws, IFRS, and other statutory regulations.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and financial analysis tools.


Person Specification:


	Excellent attention to detail, accuracy, and organizational skills.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
	High level of integrity and professionalism.
	Effective communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
	Results-oriented, with the ability to meet deadlines under pressure.
	A proactive mindset with a continuous improvement approach.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 / month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;jobs@jevconsulting.com.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Accountant Job at JEV Consulting




        
    

        
        JEV Consulting - Our client, one of the major foremost Dental Clinics, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location: Festac, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The accountant will be responsible for managing the company’s financial records, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements, preparing financial statements, and providing strategic financial insights to support decision-making.
	The ideal candidate must possess strong analytical skills, financial acumen, and the ability to thrive in a dynamic work environment.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Prepare, examine, and analyze accounting records, financial statements, and other financial reports to ensure accuracy and compliance with accepted standards and regulations.
	Manage accounts payable and receivable functions, including vendor reconciliation and invoice processing.
	Oversee payroll processing, tax remittances, and statutory deductions (PAYE, VAT, WHT, etc.).
	Maintain and reconcile general ledger accounts and ensure timely month-end and year-end closures.
	Monitor cash flow, forecast financial trends, and advise management on cost optimization and revenue enhancement strategies.
	Support audits (internal and external) and ensure full compliance with financial policies and procedures.
	Liaise with banks, auditors, and regulatory authorities as required.
	Assist in budget preparation, performance analysis, and variance reporting.
	Ensure accurate financial documentation and record-keeping for all transactions.


Qualifications and Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or any related discipline.
	Student member of ICAN, ACCA, or equivalent is desirable but not mandatory.
	2–5 years relevant experience, preferably in a hospital or health industry.
	Strong knowledge of accounting software (e.g., QuickBooks, Sage, SAP, or Tally).
	In-depth understanding of Nigerian tax laws, IFRS, and other statutory regulations.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and financial analysis tools.


Person Specification:


	Excellent attention to detail, accuracy, and organizational skills.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
	High level of integrity and professionalism.
	Effective communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
	Results-oriented, with the ability to meet deadlines under pressure.
	A proactive mindset with a continuous improvement approach.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Candidates living at Festac, Mile 2, Okota, Ago, Amuwo Odofin, Satelite Town and its environs are preferred for this role for proximity’s sake.

	Develop, plan, and execute FruityLife&rsquo;s social media strategy across all platforms.
	Createcaptions that align with the brand voice.
	Create and manage the social media content calendar and ensure timely publishing.
	Monitor social media trends and recommend innovative campaign ideas to drive engagement.
	Respond to comments, messages, and customer inquiries promptly to enhance customer experience.
	Collaborate with the design, production, and marketing teams to support campaigns, product launches, and promotions.
	Track performance metrics and prepare monthly analytics reports with actionable insights.
	Support influencer partnerships and digital campaigns to increase visibility and conversions.
	Undertake other duties as assigned.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications with experience.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;faith@workinnigeria.org&nbsp;using &quot;SCM&quot; as the&nbsp;subject of the email.",

Social Media Manager Job at KarFerry Limited




        
    

        
        KarFerry Limited is a Recruitment Company with branches in Island and Ikeja.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Social Media Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Develop, plan, and execute FruityLife’s social media strategy across all platforms.
	Createcaptions that align with the brand voice.
	Create and manage the social media content calendar and ensure timely publishing.
	Monitor social media trends and recommend innovative campaign ideas to drive engagement.
	Respond to comments, messages, and customer inquiries promptly to enhance customer experience.
	Collaborate with the design, production, and marketing teams to support campaigns, product launches, and promotions.
	Track performance metrics and prepare monthly analytics reports with actionable insights.
	Support influencer partnerships and digital campaigns to increase visibility and conversions.
	Undertake other duties as assigned.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications with experience.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "SCM" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
